fields.description:
## Why

External creation paths have different semantics from local facades. A Jira issue materialized locally is a jira creation, while an existing local ticket later pushed to Jira keeps its original channel. NDJSON import creates a fresh local record through import while source_* continues to describe the prior store. Historical Jira records need a conservative answer without rewriting shared event history.

## What

- Add creation_channel=jira to the CREATE data assembled only by the inbound Jira materialization path in src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound_records.py. That direct writer calls validate_creation_channel("jira") from reducer/_version.py before writing because it bypasses create_core. For placeholder identities, _ensure_inbound_assignee_identity passes creation_channel="jira" through rebar.ensure_identity_for -> identity.ensure_identity_for -> create_identity_core -> create_core. The dependency story owns these signatures with python defaults; this story owns only the Jira argument at the inbound boundary.
- Preserve the original cli, mcp, or python channel when a local ticket is later bound or pushed outbound to Jira; sync direction must not rewrite genesis provenance.
- Have src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py invoke the shared library creation path with the internal import channel. The newly created local ticket reports import even when the exported source record contains another creation_channel. Existing source_id/source_created_at/source_author/source_env behavior remains unchanged.
- Add _project_legacy_creation_channel(state: dict) -> None in reducer/_processors.py. It is a no-op only when state has a concrete recorded value (cli, mcp, python, jira, or import) or creation_channel_inferred=true. An absent, None, or provisional unknown value without the marker is unresolved legacy input: the helper reads state["ticket_id"], state.get("author"), plus state.get("env_id"), then sets either jira with creation_channel_inferred=true or unknown with the marker absent. Call it at the end of process_create after ticket_id/author/env_id and recorded CREATE data have folded. This state-shaped interface is shared unchanged by process_snapshot in child 568c. Define LEGACY_JIRA_AUTHOR="reconciler" plus LEGACY_JIRA_ENV_ID="reconciler" in reducer/_version.py. inbound_translate._event_meta performs the absolute package import from rebar.reducer._version inside the function body for its default author/env values, following the existing late-import pattern, and the reducer helper imports the same constants normally. The absolute rebar.* import is compatible with the existing isolated spec loader and does not rely on sibling rebar_reconciler package context; extend the spec-load contract test to pin it. When creation_channel is missing, infer jira only if ticket_id starts with "jira-", author equals LEGACY_JIRA_AUTHOR, and env_id equals LEGACY_JIRA_ENV_ID. Missing/None or env-overridden historical signals are mismatches. On a match set creation_channel=jira plus creation_channel_inferred=true; on every mismatch set creation_channel=unknown and omit the marker.
- Base inference only on creation-stable state. Do not inspect current tags, current Jira bindings, source_* fields, comments, or mutable reconciler state.
- Add positive and one-signal-at-a-time negative tests so a locally crafted title/tag/binding cannot trigger inference.
- Document recorded versus inferred Jira provenance, the import/source_* distinction, and the diagnostic trust boundary: creation_channel_inferred is heuristic audit metadata, not a security attestation. Extend test_schema_coverage.py to pin that statement.

## Approach Rationale

New external writers declare their channel because they know it exactly. Historical inference is limited to the deterministic ID/author/environment conjunction already produced by the legacy reconciler: inbound_translate._jira_key_to_local_id generates jira-* IDs; _event_meta uses the shared LEGACY_JIRA_AUTHOR and LEGACY_JIRA_ENV_ID constants, each exactly "reconciler", as defaults. A function-local absolute import from rebar.reducer._version is the chosen sharing seam because spec-loaded reconciler modules already resolve installed rebar.* package imports through established late-import paths; a contract test loads inbound_translate through the isolated loader, calls _event_meta, and asserts both defaults, preventing either resolution failure or duplicated-literal drift. Requiring all three signals prioritizes zero false positives; overridden, missing, or incomplete legacy metadata intentionally returns unknown.

## Scope

IN: Jira inbound CREATE, Jira-side-effect identity creation, outbound non-rewrite behavior, NDJSON import, full-log legacy Jira inference, negative controls, export/import semantics, and documentation.

OUT: guessing historical local channels, mutating old events, treating Jira binding as creation, executing SNAPSHOT-only legacy inference (the helper interface intentionally supports it; child 568c owns the call and tests), compaction, and new filtering/reporting surfaces.

## Files

- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/apply_inbound_records.py and inbound_translate.py call sites: recorded Jira channel and side-effect identities.
- src/rebar/_io/import_ndjson.py and _io/_provenance.py: recorded import channel while preserving source_*.
- src/rebar/reducer/_version.py and src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/inbound_translate.py: shared exact legacy Jira constants used through an absolute package import by producer defaults and reducer inference.
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_env_aliasing.py: isolated spec-load coverage for the shared constants and exact defaults.
- src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py: shared deterministic legacy projection helper.
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_creation_channel.py: Jira inbound/outbound and inference controls.
- tests/interfaces/io/test_creation_channel.py and test_export_ndjson.py: import/export behavior.
- docs/event-schema.md, docs/cli-reference.md, and docs/output-schemas.md: Jira/import channel semantics in event, CLI, and public output contracts.

## Dependencies

Depends on the local-contract story because it supplies the enum, writer validation, reducer field, and public output contract.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A newly materialized inbound Jira issue and any placeholder identity created by that path persist validated creation_channel=jira, independent of reconciler author/env overrides; the direct writer rejects an off-vocabulary injected value. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_creation_channel.py -k inbound_recorded.
- [ ] Binding or pushing an existing cli/mcp/python ticket to Jira never changes its stored or projected creation_channel. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_creation_channel.py -k outbound.
- [ ] NDJSON import persists creation_channel=import on the new local CREATE, preserves source_* provenance, and does not copy the exported source ticket channel into the new local channel. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/io/test_creation_channel.py -k import.
- [ ] NDJSON export includes creation_channel plus creation_channel_inferred when present, using the TicketState output contract without a parallel serializer rule, and the full export suite remains green. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/io/test_export_ndjson.py.
- [ ] A legacy full-log ticket with no recorded channel projects jira plus creation_channel_inferred=true only when jira-* ID, reconciler author, and reconciler env_id all match exactly. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_creation_channel.py -k legacy_positive.
- [ ] Removing or changing each inference signal independently produces creation_channel=unknown with no inference marker; tags, bindings, source_* metadata, and titles never affect the decision. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_creation_channel.py -k legacy_negative.
- [ ] Existing import idempotency and reconciler inbound behavior remain green. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/interfaces/io/test_import_idempotency.py tests/interfaces/io/test_import_ndjson.py tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_inbound_leaf_bodies.py.
- [ ] Isolated spec-loading of inbound_translate resolves the shared legacy constants plus exact defaults without sibling-package context. TicketState output regression remains green. docs/output-schemas.md states that creation_channel_inferred is diagnostic metadata rather than a security attestation. Prove with uv run pytest -q tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_env_aliasing.py tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_outputs.py tests/interfaces/contracts/test_schema_coverage.py && make lint.
